prompt.content: I want to identify whether customers on smaller plans are consistently approaching their monthly plan limits, indicating upsell opportunity. Using the data_usage_daily table, calculate the percentage of active usage user-months where the total data_consumed_mb exceeded 80% of the plan allowance for users on plans with <=10GB data limit. Note that the monthly usage cycle starts on the billing_cycle_day each month. Perform the calculation for all months where the billing cycle started in 2024. To account for boundary months due to offsets between activation_date/contract_end_date and billing_cycle_day, exclude any billing month that starts in the same calendar month as the activation_date or contract_end_date. Join data_usage_daily to subscribers, then to subscriber_plans, then to subscription_plans to get plan details. Only include usage records that fall within the activation_date to contract_end_date range (inclusive). Report the total number of user-months in the sample, the number of user-months where the total data_consumed_mb exceeded 80% of their plan's allowance, and the percent which this figure represents to 2 decimals. Use a 1024MB/GB conversion. Return the SQLite code used for the analysis as well as the final calculated results.

rubrics.name: Reports the number of user-months in the sample to be 639 +/- 1. (ver_a36c4aa1f899454d8ce3d527e5750501)

messages.content: Reports the number of user-months in the sample to be 639 +/- 1.

rubrics.name: Reports the number of user-months where the total data consumed exceeded 80%... (ver_d4109c4e4bff447588a538431b7ad2c3)

messages.content: Reports the number of user-months where the total data consumed exceeded 80% of the plan's allowance to be 230 +/- 1.

rubrics.name: Reports that the percent of user-months where the user consumed over 80% of... (ver_e0896148d3ea451dad55fe2c1f26303f)

messages.content: Reports that the percent of user-months where the user consumed over 80% of their data allowance to be 35.99% +/- 0.05%.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
number_of_user_months  number_of_exceeded_user_months  percent_exceeded_user_months
                   639                           230.0                         35.99

```sql
WITH daily AS (
  SELECT
    d.subscriber_id,
    d.usage_date,
    d.data_consumed_mb,
    sp.activation_date,
    sp.contract_end_date,
    sp.billing_cycle_day,
    p.data_allowance_gb,
  
    CASE -- figure out billing cycle start date
      WHEN CAST(strftime('%d', d.usage_date) AS INTEGER) >= sp.billing_cycle_day
        THEN DATE(d.usage_date, 'start of month', '+' || (sp.billing_cycle_day - 1) || ' days')
      ELSE DATE(d.usage_date, 'start of month', '-1 month', '+' || (sp.billing_cycle_day - 1) || ' days')
    END AS current_billing_month,
  
    -- calc months for filtering later on
    DATE(sp.activation_date, 'start of month') AS activation_month,
    DATE(sp.contract_end_date, 'start of month') AS contract_end_month
  
  FROM data_usage_daily d
  JOIN subscribers s ON s.subscriber_id = d.subscriber_id
  JOIN subscriber_plans sp ON sp.subscriber_id = d.subscriber_id
  JOIN subscription_plans p ON p.plan_id = sp.plan_id
  ),
filtered AS (
  SELECT
    subscriber_id,
    current_billing_month,
    data_consumed_mb,
    data_allowance_gb
  FROM daily
  WHERE
    current_billing_month BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-12-31'
    AND usage_date >= activation_date -- edge case filter
    AND (usage_date <= contract_end_date OR contract_end_date IS NULL) -- edge case filter
    AND strftime('%Y-%m', current_billing_month) > strftime('%Y-%m', activation_date) -- exclude boundary months
    AND (contract_end_date IS NULL OR strftime('%Y-%m', current_billing_month) < strftime('%Y-%m', contract_end_date)) -- exclude boundary months
    AND data_allowance_gb != 'unlimited'
    AND CAST(data_allowance_gb AS REAL) <= 10
  ),
--Now we can groupby user-month on filtered table
grouped AS (
  SELECT
    subscriber_id,
    current_billing_month,
    CASE
      -- data_allowance_gb is guaranteed to be same value - max is fine as agg func here
      WHEN SUM(data_consumed_mb)/1024.0/CAST(MAX(data_allowance_gb) AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1.0
      ELSE 0.0
    END AS exceeded
  FROM filtered
  GROUP BY subscriber_id, current_billing_month
  )
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS number_of_user_months,
  SUM(exceeded) AS number_of_exceeded_user_months,
  ROUND(100*SUM(exceeded)/COUNT(*), 2) AS percent_exceeded_user_months
  FROM grouped;
```
